title: UCP-1 (Abcam ab10983) immunohistochemical protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6ila0","text":"Increasing energy expenditure by stimulating thermogenesis through activation of brown adipose tissue (BAT) and/or induction of browning of white adipose tissue (WAT) is considered a promising strategy to treat/prevent obesity and related metabolic diseases. Whereas WAT is adapted to store energy as triglycerides, BAT produces heat (non-shivering thermogenesis). In brown adipocytes, the uncoupling protein-1 (UCP-1) regulates conversion of energy into heat by uncoupling ATP production from mitochondrial respiration. Also in WAT adaptive UCP-1 positive adipocytes (brown in white: brite or beige) can arise, predominantly in subcutaneous (s) WAT. This browning of WAT is enhanced by exposure to cold temperatures. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"6r6ed","text":"-To dissolve the blocking reagent in TNB, heat TNB blocking buffer to 60°C for 1 hour with stirring. Aliquot over 15 ml tubes. TNB may be stored for up to 1 month at -20°C.\n-Pre-incubate the secondary goat anti-rabbit-biotin couped antibody with the pre-immune serum 1 day before use.\n-Harris hemtaoxylin: 5 sec is a guideline. If solution is fresh, it is best to reduce the time, otherwise sections will be overstained. The Harris hematoxylin work solution has to be replaced every two weeks and filtered daily.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"8cb1a","text":"-Turn on 37°C oven\n-Prewarm slide box and 0.1% CaCl2 solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":51,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7llb","text":"-Make sure TNB is stored at -20°C","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3j2ib","text":"-Xylene: exposure to xylene via inhalation, eyes, ingestion or skin contact. Xylene causes health eﬀects from both acute (\u003c14 days) and chronic (\u003e365 days) exposure. In addition, the health eﬀect will be diﬀerent depending on the amount a person has been exposed to. Therefore, to avoid exposure to xylene via the skin, special xylene-resistant gloves are worn during xylene handling. Therefore, hydration and rehydration steps and the mounting of the slides are done in a fume hood due to xylene.\n-Harris Hematoxylin: special waste container, flammable, eye and skin irritation\n-DAB: very carcinogenic, mutagenic compound. Needs to be worked with in a separate fume hood with separate containers, separate waste and separate glassware and utensils.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: 10 min 1x TBS (37°C)*10x Tris-buffered-saline (TBS): - 200 ml Tris-HCl pH 7.5 1 M - 600 ml NaCl 5 M - 8 ml Tween - Stir- Add AD to 2,000 ml *1x TBS: mix 100 ml 10x TBS with 900 ml AD Tween 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P1379-500ml

7 min Trypsin 37°C 2% solution in Trypsin buffer (dilution 1/80) Trypsin buffer (1L): -50 ml 1 M Tris-HCl pH 7.8 (0.05 M)-30 ml 5 M NaCl (0.15 M)-1g CaCl 2 (0.1%)-Dissolve in 800 ml AD-Add AD to 1L Trypsin from porcine pancreas Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #T7409
Peroxidase Block: 200 ml methanol + 600 µl H 2 O 2 30% (add just before slides go into methanol solution) Hydrogen peroxide 30% Merck Millipore (EMD Millipore) Catalog #822287.1000

45 min pre-immune goat serum 1/5 in TNB TNB (1L):-100 ml 1 M Tris-HCl pH 7.5 (0.1 M)-30 ml 5 M NaCl (0.15 M)-5g blocking reagent (0.5%) from TSA BT kit Pre-immune goat serum Contributed by users Catalog #X090710 TSA BT amplification kit Perkin Elmer Catalog #NEL700001
Overnight polyclonal rabbit anti-murine UCP-1 1:200 dilution in TNB at room temperature Polyclonal rabbit anti-mouse UCP-1 antibody Abcam Catalog #ab10983
3 x 5 min TNT TNT (1L):-100 ml 1 M Tris-HCl pH 7.5 (0.1 M)-30 ml 5 M NaCl (0.15 M)-0.4 ml Tween-20 (0.04%)

45 min goat anti-rabbit-biotin couped secondary antibody 1/300 + 10% pre-immune mouse serum (all diluted in TNB) Polyclonal Goat Anti-Rabbit Immunoglobulins/Biotinylated * 1 ml Contributed by users Catalog #E043201 Mouse serum Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #M5905-10ML
30 min streptavidin-coupled peroxidase (PO) 1/100 in TNB (reagent found in TSA BT kit)
8 min biotinyl tyramide (BT) 1/50 diluted in amplification diluent (both reagents supplied in TSA BT kit)
3 x 5 min 1x TRIS 10x TRIS (1L):-500 ml 1M Tris-HCl pH 7.5-500 ml AD 1x TRIS (1L):-100 ml 10x TRIS-900 ml AD

DAB (200 ml 1x TRIS + 65 µl H 2 O 2 30% added fresh)-Transfer a small amount of DAB to a 0.4 - 0.7 ml microcentrifuge tube (fill only the cone)-Add DAB to cilinder containing 200 ml TRIS-Put parafilm on top of cilinder and seal it-Mix solution by hand until most of the DAB is dissolved-Filter-Pour solution into designated container for slides-Add H 2 O 2 to solution just before use. 3,3-DIAMINOBENZIDINE.4HCl.xH2O Pure 98% * 5 g Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #32750-5G
Harris Hematoxylin 5 sec Solution:-100 ml Harris Hematoxylin-100 ml AD-2 ml glacial acetic acid Harris Hematoxylin GURR® mercury free 1L VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #351945S
Mount coverslip with DPX DPX Merck Millipore (EMD Millipore) Catalog #1.00579.0500
creator: Bianca Hemmeryckx
